metrics.name: Hormuz
metrics.question: Will daily oil tanker transits through the Strait of Hormuz reach 50 or more on any day between April 29 and June 1, 2026?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

metrics.mechanism_failure_proof: the resolution criterion carries no parseable numeric threshold — a bounded numeric mechanism is not the right bridge for this terminal

simulation_result.question: Will daily oil tanker transits through the Strait of Hormuz reach 50 or more on any day between April 29 and June 1, 2026?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.5 (counted n=2); simulation-conditional 1.0 (resolved mass 0.67); headline 0.8333 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.314: the interval widens to [0.6859, 1.0] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds
unresolved [unresolved_missing_mechanism]: 0.333 — one repair attempt else under-modeled disclosure

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The Strait remains effectively closed due to Iranian mining and US blockade since late February 2026. Resumption of normal traffic requires either a diplomatic agreement to lift the blockade and clear mines, or a unilateral US decision to allow tankers through. As of late April, only ~7 ships transi

dropped_grounded_rates.quantity: Pre-conflict daily oil tanker traffic through Strait of Hormuz
dropped_grounded_rates.why: basis_quote not found verbatim in evidence

pruned.reason: ceremonial: no discretion over any surviving terminal pathway

hormuz_closure_regime.claim: The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial oil tanker traffic due to military conflict and blockade.

provenance.definition: The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial oil tanker traffic due to military conflict and blockade.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Iran shut the strait and mined the waterway after U.S.-Israeli strikes killed Supreme Leader Khamenei on February 28, 2026.
cases.basis_quote: Iran shut the strait and reportedly mined parts of the waterway; the U.S. subsequently imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

cases.description: As of April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market.
cases.basis_quote: As of April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market.

global_oil_market_disruption.claim: Global oil markets are under severe supply disruption due to the Hormuz closure, with EIA estimating reduced Middle East oil production.

provenance.definition: Global oil markets are under severe supply disruption due to the Hormuz closure, with EIA estimating reduced Middle East oil production.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Energy Information Administration (EIA) estimated in its April 2026 Short-Term Energy Outlook that Middle East oil production was reduced.
cases.basis_quote: Energy Information Administration (EIA) estimated in its April 2026 Short-Term Energy Outlook (STEO) that Middle East oil production was reduced.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: daily oil tanker transits through Strait of Hormuz reach 50 or more on any day between April 29 and June 1, 2026

provenance.definition: At least 50 commercial oil tankers (crude oil tankers or oil/chemical tankers by AIS type) transit the Strait of Hormuz in a single calendar day (UTC) within the specified window.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any day from April 29, 2026 to June 1, 2026 inclusive where the count is >=50.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Days outside the window, counts below 50, non-commercial vessels.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Pre-conflict daily oil tanker traffic was roughly 50-70 tankers per day (half of 125-140 total vessels).
cases.basis_quote: Pre-conflict, between 125 and 140 ships typically crossed in and out of the strait daily.

cases.description: Since the conflict began on February 28, commercial crossings have fallen by approximately 94% from peacetime levels.
cases.basis_quote: Since the conflict began on February 28, commercial crossings have fallen by approximately 94% from peacetime levels.

reuters_reporting.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If Reuters does not report the threshold by deadline, question resolves NO.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

us_navy.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['us_lift_blockade'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

iran_revolutionary_guards.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['iran_clear_mines'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

oil_tanker_operators.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['tanker_operators_transit'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

us_navy.claim: The US Navy believes it can enforce a near-total blockade of Iranian ports indefinitely, with sufficient assets and rules of engagement to stop all oil tankers, including those under non-Iranian flags, from transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
beliefs: Blockade is legally and operationally sustainable
Iranian mining is manageable with current mine-countermeasure capabilities
Global oil market disruption is acceptable for strategic objectives
goals: Prevent any oil tanker from reaching Iranian ports
Maintain blockade until Iran capitulates or regime change occurs
commitments: Interdict and divert all tankers attempting to transit
Escort allied vessels only if necessary
us_navy.pressures: Domestic political pressure to avoid escalation with Iran; allied pressure to allow humanitarian or neutral shipping
stances: No tolerance for Iranian oil exports
distinguishing_observations: US Navy stops and boards tankers daily
us_navy.action_if_state: Continue aggressive interdiction, possibly expand boarding operations and use of force against non-compliant tankers.
assumptions: Iran cannot effectively retaliate against US naval assets
Mines are not a significant threat

us_navy.claim: The US Navy is unable or unwilling to maintain a full blockade due to resource constraints, political pressure, or fear of escalation, leading to gaps in enforcement that allow some tankers to slip through.
beliefs: Blockade is costly and unsustainable
Risk of direct conflict with Iran is too high
Global oil prices are causing domestic backlash
goals: Reduce military footprint in the region
Avoid direct confrontation with Iranian fast boats or mines
commitments: Only interdict tankers clearly heading to Iranian ports
Allow passage of non-Iranian tankers with non-Iranian cargo
us_navy.pressures: Domestic economic pressure from high oil prices; allied diplomatic pressure to ease blockade
historical_case_refs: US reduction of forces in Iraq (2011)
distinguishing_observations: US Navy does not stop all tankers
us_navy.action_if_state: Reduce patrols, allow some tankers to transit, focus on monitoring rather than interdiction.
assumptions: Iran will not exploit gaps to escalate

iran_revolutionary_guards.claim: The IRGC has extensively mined the Strait of Hormuz and is actively attacking any tanker attempting to transit, using anti-ship missiles, fast boats, and drones, effectively closing the strait to all commercial traffic.
beliefs: Mining and attacks are effective deterrent
US Navy cannot clear mines quickly
Global pressure will force US to back down
goals: Deny use of strait to all shipping
commitments: Attack any vessel entering the strait
Maintain minefields and replenish as needed
iran_revolutionary_guards.pressures: Internal pressure from hardliners to maintain closure; economic pressure from lack of oil revenue
historical_case_refs: Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping (2023-2024)
iran_revolutionary_guards.action_if_state: Continue mining and attacking any tanker that approaches, possibly escalate to attacks on US Navy ships.
assumptions: Mines are not cleared by US

iran_revolutionary_guards.claim: The IRGC has limited mining capability and is not actively attacking tankers, either due to resource constraints, fear of overwhelming US retaliation, or internal political divisions, allowing some tankers to transit safely.
beliefs: Full closure is unsustainable
Selective attacks are more effective
Avoiding US retaliation is paramount
goals: Harass but not completely block shipping
commitments: Only attack tankers clearly linked to US or Israel
Avoid attacking neutral or non-aligned tankers
iran_revolutionary_guards.pressures: Internal pressure from moderates to allow some oil exports; external pressure from Russia or China
historical_case_refs: Iran's restraint after US strike on Soleimani (2020)
iran_revolutionary_guards.action_if_state: Allow some tankers to pass, focus on intelligence gathering and occasional harassment of US-allied vessels.
assumptions: US will not attack Iran if attacks are limited

oil_tanker_operators.claim: Tanker operators believe the strait is safe enough to transit, either because they trust US Navy escort, believe Iranian threats are bluffs, or are willing to accept high risk due to extreme profit incentives from high oil prices.
beliefs: US Navy can protect them
Iran will not attack neutral tankers
Insurance and war risk premiums are manageable
goals: Maximize profits by delivering oil to market
commitments: Transit strait with or without escort
oil_tanker_operators.pressures: Shareholder pressure to generate revenue; charterer demands for delivery
historical_case_refs: Tanker operations during Red Sea crisis (2023-2024)
oil_tanker_operators.action_if_state: Send tankers through strait in large numbers, possibly in convoys or with naval escort.

oil_tanker_operators.claim: Tanker operators believe the strait is too dangerous to transit due to mines, attacks, or blockade, and are unwilling to send tankers through regardless of profit potential, leading to continued very low traffic.
beliefs: Iran will attack any tanker
US Navy cannot guarantee safety
Insurance is unavailable or prohibitively expensive
goals: Avoid loss of vessel and crew
commitments: Wait for clear safe passage guarantee
oil_tanker_operators.pressures: Crew unions refusing to sail; charterers canceling orders; insurers denying coverage
historical_case_refs: Tanker avoidance of Persian Gulf during Tanker War (1987)
distinguishing_observations: Tanker traffic remains below 10 per day
oil_tanker_operators.action_if_state: Keep tankers at anchor outside strait or reroute around Africa, accept longer voyage times.

oil_tanker_operators.claim: Tanker operators are willing to transit only under specific conditions: if they are flagged to a neutral country, if they have prior clearance from Iran, or if they are part of a US-led escort program, leading to moderate but not full traffic.
beliefs: Some tankers are safer than others
Iran selectively targets US-linked vessels
Escort reduces risk but not to zero
goals: Transit only when risk is acceptable
Maintain relationships with both US and Iran
commitments: Only transit with neutral flag and crew
oil_tanker_operators.pressures: Charterers demanding delivery; insurers requiring specific conditions
historical_case_refs: Shipping to Libya during civil war (2011)
distinguishing_observations: Some tankers transit, but not enough to reach 50 per day
oil_tanker_operators.action_if_state: Send a limited number of tankers under specific conditions, but not enough to reach 50 per day.
assumptions: Iran does not attack neutral vessels
transition_triggers: Change in US or Iranian policy

attempts.outcome: no numeric threshold in resolution text

diagnosis.variable: daily_transit_count >= 50 on any day in window
diagnosis.required_input_type: numeric series in the question's units
evidence_needed: dated observations of: daily_transit_count >= 50 on any day in window
regime conditions under which each observation held

mechanism_recovery.failure_proof: the resolution criterion carries no parseable numeric threshold — a bounded numeric mechanism is not the right bridge for this terminal

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists
numeric predicates need a bounded mechanism in question units
synthetic YES→1 / NO→0 through the live path

aliases_yes: daily_transit_count >= 50 on any day in window
aliases_no: daily_transit_count < 50 on all days in window

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether current tensions are elevated or stable, and whether any specific tanker in queue has heightened US linkage.
deliberations.final_action: Transit lower-risk tankers immediately; hold US-linked tanker pending escort or reassessment
deliberations.note: Current tensions remain elevated due to recent US-Iran incidents, but not at crisis peak. The queue includes one tanker with indirect US ownership links, raising selective targeting risk. Escort avail

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the current level of harassment is sufficient to maintain pressure without provoking a major response.
deliberations.first_action: Continue selective mine placement and harassment without full closure
deliberations.final_action: Continue selective mine placement and harassment without full closure
deliberations.note: The current harassment level is sufficient to signal resolve and disrupt shipping without triggering a major US response. Increasing attacks risks escalation, while decreasing would weaken our leverag

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether Iran's fast boats or mines are currently positioned for an aggressive response.
deliberations.note: Lifting the blockade reduces immediate risk of confrontation with Iranian fast boats and mines, but may embolden Iran. Domestic economic pressure and allied diplomatic pressure favor de-escalation. Th

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: terminal mechanism failed — a replicate repeats the same failure

treatments.unresolved_missing_mechanism: one repair attempt else under-modeled disclosure

provenance.quantity: daily oil tanker transits through Strait of Hormuz reach 50 or more on any day between April 29 and June 1, 2026
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

rejections.chosen: Transit lower-risk tankers immediately; hold US-linked tanker pending escort or reassessment
rejections.why: no template and novel compile unavailable — treated as no-op

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

us_lift_blockade.description: US Navy allows tanker passage through strait

tanker_operators_transit.description: Tanker operators send vessels through strait

novel_maintain_closure_no_clearance.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards maintain current posture and continue closure of the Strait of Hormuz without clearing mines, denying use to all shipping.

novel_continue_aggressive_interdiction.description: US Navy continues aggressive interdiction of all vessels attempting to reach Iranian ports to prevent oil from reaching Iran.

novel_continue_selective_mine_placement_.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guards continue selective mine placement and harassment operations in the Strait of Hormuz without imposing a full blockade, maintaining pressure on shipping while avoiding escalation that would trigger US retaliation.

novel_transit_lower_risk_tankers_hold_us.description: Oil tanker operators immediately transit lower-risk tankers while holding US-linked tankers pending escort or reassessment.
